title: Clan
content:
{{Top|leg}}
{{MoreSources}}
{{Citation}}
A '''clan''' was the familial and often political organization that made up a society. Clan-based cultures included [[Rodian/Legends|Rodians]], [[Bothan/Legends|Bothans]],<Ref name="guide">''[[Shadows of the Empire Planets Guide]]''</ref> [[Witches of Dathomir]],<Ref name="TCoPL">''[[The Courtship of Princess Leia]]''</ref> [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]],<Ref name="TOR">''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]''</ref> [[Noghri/Legends|Noghri]],<ref name="TLC">''[[The Last Command]]''</ref> [[Trandoshan/Legends|Trandoshans]],<Ref name="SWG:RotW">''[[Star Wars Galaxies: Rage of the Wookiees]]''</ref> [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiees]],<ref name="RotS:TVD">''[[Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith: The Visual Dictionary]]''</ref> [[Codru-Ji]],<ref name="Legacy 13">[[Legacy (2006) 13|''Legacy'' (2006) 13]]</ref> and [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'leks]].<ref name="XWRS11">[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 11|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 11]]</ref>

==Rodian clans==
Since ancient times, Rodians were a tribal society. A clan was ruled by a [[Chief Protector]], and associated with some land and/or enclave; enclaves grew later into cities, and some absorbed other smaller enclaves as they grew. Clan members were sometimes wholly dedicated to some profession, organization or criminal syndicate.<ref name="guide" />

In later times, clans were also political entities, and were often allies or enemies with each other; their skirmishes sometimes escalated to [[Rodian Clan Wars|planetary war]]. Affiliation was often a private matter that was not usually obvious or publicly shared; Rodians who revealed their clan risked being exposed to Rodians of enemy clans that could be present. Warfare, geography and other factors shaped the development of each clan; some weaker clans remained isolated in their ancestral lands in the jungle, and limited to older technology. Newer clans were usually small groups that split from traditional clans, often supporting radical views; as these emerged in the cities, they possessed no lands.<ref name="guide" />

The [[Chattza clan/Legends|Chattza Clan]] emerged dominant after the Rodian Clan Wars.<ref name="guide" />

==Bothan clans==
Representatives from the clans comprised the [[Combined Bothan Clans|Bothan Council]]. By the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], the [[Combined Bothan Clans|Bothan Council]] was composed of 608 registered Clans, with 53 new Clans petitioning for legal status.<Ref name="guide" /> During the [[New Republic era]], the Clans were collectively known as the [[Combined Bothan Clans]].<ref name="SotP">''[[Specter of the Past]]''</ref>

One could tell a Bothan's Clan by the suffix in their name. The "Lon" in [[Tereb Ab'Lon]] for instance denoted he was either from the Ilon or Alon Clan. A Bothan without such a suffix was usually considered a dishonored outcast.

Several Bothan clans assisted the [[Dressellian/Legends|Dressellians]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] by supplying them with strategic information and thousands of [[Slugthrower rifle/Legends|slugthrower rifles]].<ref name="EGWT">''[[The Essential Guide to Weapons and Technology]]''</ref>
==Wookiee clans==
[[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiees]] also belonged to clans, such as [[Chevappa]], [[Chyakk clan/Legends|Chyakk]], [[Gkrur clan/Legends|Gkrur]], [[Grrobahrr]], [[Kachirho clan|Kachirho]], [[Myyydril]], [[Nysshyyyk]], [[Puurrgerr]], [[Strongfist clan|Strongfist]] and [[Tojj clan|Tojj]].
